item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that the image could not be properly sourced and is assumed to be non-compliant. As such, it has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If Doctor ██████ does, in the unlikely event, go skateboarding with SCP-2014, he needs to buy SCP-2014 a TECHDECK™.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is this a reference to the 90s skater game Gex, about a gecko that skates, or is the universe skating-gecko shaped?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Murder, theft, and indecent exposure". This is pretty funny and leaves a lot to the imagination, but not too much. This is great. Although it's four years old, I wish there was more to it. Incident reports maybe? But don't take my words too seriously, I rarely ever know what I'm talking about.
Good work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Marvin's broken.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find this article pretty fun, and the gecko is cute enough that alone I probably would have upvoted. The thing is due to the mention of it's wife, and kids I'm pretty sure this is an eldritch abomination going through a mid-life crisis. Which just makes this a hundred times better, and makes me want to upvote it even more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I personally think this article has hidden horror aspect. The Foundation clearly underestimate danger coming from this SCP. He's telekinetic, right? What's his limit? A guy with a skateboard or the Sun? He's an elder creature that won't hesitate to destroy us all in one fell swoop.
While we find him "gnarly and cute", Foundation's personall probably flipping their shit tables about this guy.

Plus he is cute and cool. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be real, he's technically ​not a speaking animal, since he's a telekineticly wrighting animal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This gets my upvote for it's general gnarliness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No vote for me. If it was a -j I would probably upvot

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The article specifies it's using it's telekinesis to manipulate a pen and write responses.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I down voted this one because it's trying waaay too hard to be cute and wacky. Seriously, lines like "don't kids read their Lovecraft these days?" is just fourth-wall-breaking, wink-wink-nudge-nudge idiocy. It's grating and not even remotely clever.

That said… I did really love one thing in this. And that's the idea that this horror beyond the stars just kinda enjoys skateboarding. Like, he wasn't compelled to enjoy it do to the summoning spell; he just never happened to encounter skateboarding in all his milennia-long tentacle-squirming adventures and thought to himself, "Huh. That's pretty cool."

It reminds me a little of the Axe Man of New Orleans. An unknowable evil with a very simple, mundane hobby. Still, this was not NEARLY enough to save this from a down vote. This seriously rubbed me the wrong way. Like, every wrong way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just thought of something.
How does he talk? Does he talk with his gecko mouth, or does he talk using telepathy?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Which one?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: greatest animal scp since the intelligent spider

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know this is a really, REALLY late reply (it's a long story), but I'm back a year later to apologize.

First of all, I actually really like humorous main-list SCPs… Something about this one rubbed me the wrong way, though, so I thought "meh, might be better as a -J." My opinion on that has shifted since; must have been in a weird mood when I first read this.

Where I went wrong was "shouldn't be a main list SCP." That is and always should be a matter of community consensus, and it's a pretty awful thing to say about someone's work.

Anyway, if anybody actually sees this… Sorry that I was a dick!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Freakin' radical.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: gnarly bruh. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This scp, I just love it. The concept of a extradimensional gecko that talks like a stoner makes me very, very happy. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To quote one of our users:

Doctor ██████: Does the black moon howl?
SCP-2014: Dude, PLEASE don't remind me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man has performed horrible rituals to summon the unspeakable. He seeks to know that which must not be known. He has opened a portal to eldritch horrors, searching for a maddening power…
…And that power has found him.

…
…And it has found skateboards, and by Yog Sothoth, it's the gnarliest thing ever, dude!/
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's no good reason for me to upvote this drivel, and yet, here we are.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is amazing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know why, but I pictured the lizard with an Australian accent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"and causing several crimes, such as murder, theft and indecent exposure"

..

I just had a thought.

What if 682 is a one of his relatives?

I mean, there's no reason to assume that everything from whatever plane of existence the proto-lizard comes from is inherently hostile..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I always knew that if anything could save humanity from the wrath of interstellar gods of chaos, it would be the utterly tubular power of skating, brah. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
found this really funny, unfortunately that's why I had to -1 it.

if it was a -j it would be brilliant!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the arson, murder, and jaywalking. I'd say this has potential to be a good -J, but the skater speak kills it. Alien intelligence, wife & kids, trapped in gecko body, finds out it likes skateboarding, all cool. I'd like to see the interview rewritten as talking with something out of Lovecraft.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, uhm… Are you aware we have things like a sapient pet tornado and Curiously Buoyant Amphibian and The Mewts and Humans Go Home and a lot of other SCPs that are mostly humor, cute, etc.? Are you gonna downvote them all for not fitting your specific desire of eldritch horror?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Completely agreed. This was an enjoyable enough read, but it shouldn't be a main list SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Needs to focus more on what's "inside" the gecko, but still pretty cute.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like that indecent exposure bit was just thrown in there just because, but other than that, it's cute, unique, and interesting. I like it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When you look at every other silly talking animal/thing we have… No, there's not much of a difference here for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: People seem to think this is another talking animal even though it isn't…..it's an entity which is trapped INSIDE of the animal. A big difference really. And even if that wasn't true there's still enough to make it unique. He's a fucking cult leader, cmon! Also a flasher… I tend to like the sillier ones though, so I'm probably just biased. Also that picture is perfect in my opinion, the gecko has that mischievous look on its face that I could easily picture on an entity such as this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually like the idea of 2025 more when thinking about it affecting researchers like that. Thinking nonhuman scips are human when they clearly are not.

And sorry Weizhong, I'm a fan of Redwall, but I'm not a fan of your silly squirrels and their silly fake accents. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea of an Eldritch Abomination having a wife and kid who inherently depend on the sacrifices he gets kinda made me want to lie down and rethink some aspects of life.

Also, skateboarder gecko. Upvoted, without hesitation.

edit: Could've sworn I hit the t key.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm really not feeling this. This would be a lot better of as a -J. Okay, maybe not a lot better off. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I do like the idea of an extradimensional skater dood, and the interview definitely ups the interest after a fairly uninteresting description, but at the same time I feel like it's really overdoing the idea. Also you've got a "manipulte". No vote.

EDIT: SILLY PAST ME, THIS IS AMUSING AND TOTALLY WORTH A VOTE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In addition to being both gnarly and radical, this article is also totally bitchin'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I for one, enjoy squirrel knights.

-totallynotheauthorofsquirrelknights

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm with the others. This is like something out of the radical lands from Dr. McNinja, and I think it should stay there.

Needs more eldritch horror, less lol sk8rboi -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What is happening today? First squirrel knights, now a skate boarding gecko? What are these, rejected super bowl ads?

SCP-2025 is expanding, obviously.

Why yes, that was a shameless self-plug for an article I'm massively rewriting, thank you for noticing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, I really don't think we need any more random talking animals on the mainlist now, thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This pleases me.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted this before, simply for the sheet silliness of it, but looking back, I can't really say that's a good enough reason. Silly stuff is nice, and this is a good article, but I tend to (at least partially) upvote based on whether I think an SCP reflects what I want to see more of, and this doesn't meet that criterion. In three years, are we going to look back on these frivolous articles the same way we look back on things like author self-inserts now? I'm being hyperbolic, of course, but I can't upvote. +0

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What is happening today? First squirrel knights, now a skate boarding gecko? What are these, rejected super bowl ads?

IDK, reading through the comments, seems every other person is upvoting or downvoting for a completely different reason. The first half locked in a solid downvote from me, what with the skateboard competition and overall tone. Yet the interview was more interesting and I withheld my vote for a moment. Then I looked at the picture again. Nope, can't do it. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was really liking the idea of a powerful extradimensional being trapped in a cute little gecko's body, but the interview just killed it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, seconding this.

Also, I upvoted when I saw that the gecko had a skateboard. I was pumped, I was ready, I was prepared to laugh my ass off… and then I finished the article. You didn't really do anything with this awesome idea. It's a lizard with the mind of an eldritch abomination… and it talks like a stereotypical 90's skater.

And that's the joke. That's the whole thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd love this as a Joke SCP, but as it is now it does not work very well for me as a SCP. I laughed at the carnivorous eggs singing stupid songs, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Opened the page, stared at the picture for a few minutes, instantly upvoted. Love it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, I lost it at the picture. I mean, do an image search on "I has feets". That's what came to mind for me.

I had fun, I really did. But I don't know if this is the kind of thing I really want to see more of. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my heavens. It has feets! It…it just…this…so…I don't…what…

+1, my god!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hells yeah I'll upvote that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I still haven't stopped laughing since opening this page

SCP-2014 may use its skateboard for recreational purposes if good behaviour is mantained

1) "Maintained"

2) Now I'm making an ass of myself laughing hysterically

Finish reading, scroll up to vote more easily, see picture, start laughing again

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my god what

Best SCP of 2014 right here guys

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm still not sure if this is great or really, really dumb, but I think I'm gonna have to upvote either way.
